records.ety: [OF. overt, F. ouvert, p. p. of OF. ovrir, F. ouvrir, to open, of uncertain origin; cf. It. aprire, OIt. also oprire, L. aperire to open, operire to cover, deoperire to uncover. Perh. from L. aperire influenced by F. couvrir to cover. Cf. Aperient, Cover.]

defs: Open to view; public; apparent; manifest. Opposite of hidden.
quotes: Overt and apparent virtues bring forth praise.

defs: Not covert; open; public; manifest; as, an overt act of treason.
quotes: No person shall be convicted of treason unless on the testimony of two witnesses to the same overt act, or on confession in open court.
notes: ☞ In criminal law, an overt act is an open act done in pursuance and manifestation of a criminal design; the mere design or intent not being punishable without such act. In English law, market overt is an open market; a pound overt is an open, uncovered pound.
